articles.title: Why Are There Fewer Sheep on Farms?
articles.summary: Sheep are disappearing from farms in places like the Yorkshire Dales in the UK. For many years, families like Neil Heseltine's raised lots of sheep on their farms. But now, there are fewer sheep because it is hard for farmers to make money. Prices for things like food and fuel have gone up, and farmers get less help from the government. Also, people are eating less lamb and mutton than before. This means farms are changing, and some farmers are doing different work. The sheep help keep the hills green and pretty, so fewer sheep might change how the countryside looks. This could affect what we eat and how we enjoy nature.

articles.title: Why Mortgage Rates Are Going Up
articles.summary: In the UK, some banks and building societies are making mortgages more expensive. This means people who want to buy a home or change their mortgage will pay higher interest rates. The main reason is a war in the Middle East, which is making people worry about inflation. Inflation is when prices go up, and this can affect how much it costs to borrow money. Banks like Nationwide, HSBC, and Coventry Building Society have raised their rates a little bit. Experts say that if you need a new mortgage, it might be better to get it soon before rates go up more. They use something called swap rates to decide how much to charge. Even though rates are increasing, the banks say they are still trying to offer good deals to customers.

articles.title: A Scary Trip Home: British People Escape Danger in the Middle East
articles.summary: British people had to leave the Middle East quickly because there was fighting. They took a special government flight from Oman to the UK. The flight was delayed because of problems, but it finally left and landed safely in London. More than 140,000 British people in the area told the UK government they were there. One man, Fazal Chowdhury, drove from Dubai to Oman with his wife to catch the flight. They felt lucky to get a seat. Other people, like Amelia and Samuel, were on vacation in Dubai when the fighting started. They had to hide in a car park for safety. Everyone was scared but happy to be on the plane home. The UK government helped them by organizing the flight and communicating well.

articles.title: U.S. and Venezuela Become Friends Again After Big Change
articles.summary: The United States and Venezuela have agreed to be friends again and talk to each other after a long time of not getting along. This happened after a big change in Venezuela when the old leader, Nicolás Maduro, was removed from power in January. The two countries stopped being friends in 2019 because of disagreements. Now, they want to help the Venezuelan people by working together to make things better. U.S. officials visited Venezuela to talk about mining and oil, which are important for the country's money. The new leader in Venezuela, Delcy Rodríguez, says this will make their relationship stronger and help everyone be happier. This is a big shift because before, they were often arguing, but now they are trying to cooperate for peace and elections.

articles.title: States Sue President Trump Over New Tariffs
articles.summary: Some states are suing President Donald Trump because they don't like his new tariffs. Tariffs are taxes on things brought into the country from other places. Trump says these taxes help America by reducing trade deficits, which means we buy more from other countries than they buy from us. But the states, led by Oregon, Arizona, California, and New York, think Trump is using too much power. They say the tariffs will make things more expensive for families and businesses. The lawsuit happened after Trump lost a case at the Supreme Court and used a different law to put the tariffs in place. The White House says Trump is allowed to do this to help the country. This is a big legal fight about who can make rules about trade.

articles.title: How the Iran War Might Change Your Daily Life
articles.summary: The conflict in Iran is affecting people far away from the Middle East. This region is important for oil and gas, which we use for cars and heating. When there is trouble there, it can make fuel prices go up. For example, in the UK and US, petrol and diesel costs have increased a little recently. This might mean you pay more at the gas station or for heating your home. The article shows charts to explain this. It also compares this to when Russia invaded Ukraine, which caused bigger price jumps. The UK government helped people with energy bills then, and there are rules to protect consumers now. So, the war could make things more expensive, but it's not as bad as before yet.
